top_stories.title: Ping Identity redefines the identity control plane for the agentic enterprise
top_stories.summary: Ping Identity announced new platform capabilities designed to extend its identity control plane for agentic enterprise environments, where AI agents and automation systems require identity management. The update targets the growing need to secure and govern non-human identities, including AI agents and automated workflows, within enterprise IAM frameworks.

top_stories.title: Hackers exploit FortiClient EMS flaw to push infostealer malware
top_stories.summary: Hackers are exploiting an authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-35616) in FortiClient Enterprise Management Server (EMS) to deliver an undocumented credential stealer called EKZ. The attack targets enterprise environments through a FortiClient EMS authentication flaw, resulting in credential theft via previously undocumented malware.

top_stories.title: Threat Actors Exploit Critical FortiClient EMS Flaw to Deploy Credential Stealer
top_stories.summary: Threat actors are actively exploiting a critical vulnerability in FortiClient Endpoint Management Server (EMS) to deploy credential-stealing malware across managed endpoints. The attackers disguised the credential stealer payload as a legitimate Fortinet endpoint component, abusing trusted endpoint management infrastructure to facilitate the campaign.

top_stories.title: MFA Prompt Bombing: Why Your Second Factor Isn't Saving You
top_stories.summary: The article examines MFA prompt bombing attacks, where attackers bypass multi-factor authentication by repeatedly sending approval requests until users accept them. The piece addresses weaknesses in push notification-based MFA and how attackers exploit user behavior rather than stealing credentials directly.

top_stories.title: Ping Identity advances agentic security with AI governance and trusted access
top_stories.summary: Ping Identity announced new platform capabilities focused on agentic security, extending its platform to manage and govern access for AI agents and automated systems. The update addresses access management and governance for non-human identities in enterprise environments where AI agents and automation are increasingly involved in access decisions.

news.title: Cybercriminals sail away with data from 6 million Carnival customers
news.summary: Carnival Corporation confirmed a data breach affecting 6 million customers after the ShinyHunters hacking group claimed to have stolen customer records. The breach originated from a phishing/social engineering attack targeting a single employee account, leading to unauthorized access.

news.title: Zapier exploit chain shows how known anti-patterns compose into critical risk
news.summary: Token Security researchers disclosed a five-stage exploit chain that escalated a free Zapier account into write access on Zapier's public developer SDK packages and internal packages loaded in every authenticated zapier.com session. The chain involved a leaked NPM token and misconfigured AWS role, which Zapier remediated within four days of the February 12, 2026 submission by revoking the token and tightening the AWS role permissions.

news.title: Microsoft Slams Public Zero-Day Disclosures Amid GitHub Researcher Account Removal
news.summary: Microsoft publicly defended Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure (CVD) after removing a researcher's GitHub account following public zero-day disclosures. The researcher, known as Chaotic Eclipse, disclosed multiple zero-day vulnerabilities publicly without giving Microsoft prior opportunity to address them.

news.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Claude Security Plugin, Azure Priv-Esc, Kali365 MFA Bypass, FIFA Scams +15 More
news.summary: A threat bulletin covers multiple security topics including an Azure privilege escalation vulnerability and an MFA bypass technique called "Kali365." The roundup also references a Claude security plugin and various other threats across identity and non-identity domains.

news.title: Gartner(R)、Keeper Securityを全世界セキュリティソフトウェア市場の急成長企業第2位にランクイン
news.summary: Gartner ranked Keeper Security as the second fastest-growing company in the global security software market. Keeper Security is a password management and privileged access management vendor operating in the IAM space.

news.title: Can you enforce strong Active Directory password rules without frustrating users?
news.summary: Specops Software published guidance on enforcing strong Active Directory password policies while maintaining usability. The approach covers passphrases, breached password protection, and self-service password reset capabilities within Active Directory environments.

news.title: Bosses blinded by confidence about shadow AI use by workers
news.summary: An Okta survey found that more than half of organizations experienced an AI-related security incident or near miss in the past year. The report highlights a confidence gap between leadership and actual employee shadow AI usage within organizations.

news.title: XM Cyber rolls out tools to identify and revoke unused permissions across hybrid environments
news.summary: XM Cyber announced new platform capabilities focused on enforcing least-privilege access across Active Directory, Microsoft Entra, and multicloud environments. The tools are designed to identify and revoke unused permissions in hybrid environments as part of the company's continuous exposure management platform.

news.title: When your biggest security risk has never signed a contract
news.summary: Computer Weekly's Security Think Tank examines how AI agents are expanding the boundaries of identity and access management in the agentic era. The article explores new governance approaches required as non-human identities that lack formal contracts become significant security risks within IAM frameworks.

news.title: Understanding acceptMappedClaims in Microsoft Entra ID Claims Mapping Policies
news.summary: The article explains how to configure the `acceptMappedClaims` property in Microsoft Entra ID claims mapping policies to resolve the AADSTS50146 authentication error. It covers enabling custom claims for seamless authentication within Microsoft Entra ID's token issuance framework.

news.title: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing kit that breaks into Microsoft 365 accounts – no password required
news.summary: The FBI has issued a warning about a phishing kit called Kali365 that can compromise Microsoft 365 accounts without requiring a password. The kit bypasses multi-factor authentication, posing a direct threat to organizations relying on MFA as a security control for identity protection.

news.title: Healthcare embraces AI while facing an identity security gap: report
news.summary: A new report reveals that most healthcare organizations expect AI-driven attacks targeting their identity infrastructure. Fewer than one-third of these organizations express high confidence in their ability to regain control if an AI agent were to expose administrative credentials.

news.title: JumpCloud Continues Its Global Leadership in the G2 Summer Reports
news.summary: JumpCloud announced it received 141 Leader badges in the G2 Summer 2026 Grid® Reports, surpassing its previous record of 118. The recognition covers JumpCloud's identity and access management platform, which competes in categories relevant to directory services, SSO, and device management.

news.title: Omada Named Overall Leader in KuppingerCole's Leadership Compass Report for Identity Governance and Administration
news.summary: Omada Identity has been named an Overall Leader in KuppingerCole's Leadership Compass Report for Identity Governance and Administration, receiving leader recognition across the Overall, Product, Innovation, and Market categories. KuppingerCole's Leadership Compass is a widely referenced analyst report used by organizations to evaluate and compare IGA vendors.

news.title: Anthropic Expands Claude’s Enterprise Security Governance With 28 New Integrations
news.summary: Anthropic has expanded Claude's enterprise security governance capabilities with 28 new integrations, including one with Okta. The integration with Okta is mentioned as part of a broader set of security vendor partnerships rather than being a focused IAM-specific development.

news.title: What happens when security teams inherit identity
news.summary: At the Span Cyber Security Arena conference, Eric Woodruff, Chief Identity Architect at Semperis, discussed how organizations perceive identity and the challenges those perceptions create for security teams. The conversation focused on the organizational dynamics that arise when security teams take ownership of identity programs.

news.title: AI agents are creating a major security blind spot in financial services
news.summary: AI agents operating with excessive privileges are creating security and compliance risks in financial services. The issue centers on authorization and access control gaps as autonomous AI systems are granted overly broad permissions without adequate governance frameworks.

news.title: Manage machine identities: The hidden privileged access layer you need to manage
news.summary: The article addresses the growing challenge of managing machine identities as a privileged access layer within enterprise environments. It covers how automation, integrations, and workloads require authentication and permissions, resulting in a large and often unmanaged population of non-human identities.

news.title: TrapDoor Supply Chain Attack Spreads Credential-Stealing Malware via npm, PyPI, and CratesIO
news.summary: A coordinated supply chain attack campaign called TrapDoor has distributed credential-stealing malware across npm, PyPI, and Crates.io via more than 34 malicious packages spanning over 384 versions. The campaign began on May 22, 2026, with packages published in waves targeting developers across three major software ecosystems.

news.title: npm Adds 2FA-Gated Publishing and Package Install Controls Against Supply Chain Attacks
news.summary: GitHub has introduced "staged publishing" for npm, requiring maintainers to complete a 2FA challenge before packages become publicly available for installation. The feature is now generally available and aims to reduce supply chain attacks by ensuring human verification before package releases.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a data breach that CISA is still working to contain. Lawmakers from both chambers of Congress have demanded answers from CISA as the agency works to invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: AI Agents Are Shifting Identity Security Budget Dynamics
news.summary: New Omdia research examines how AI agent identities are creating distinct budget dynamics compared to traditional IAM projects. The research highlights the growing need to manage, secure, and govern AI agent identities as these projects proliferate across enterprises.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed credentials for highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems in a public GitHub repository. The leaked files reportedly included details on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, representing a significant privileged credential exposure incident.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. He admitted to conducting SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies, including attacks on IAM-adjacent targets using credential theft techniques.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older routers to mass harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens across more than 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, relying solely on token theft to gain unauthorized access to user accounts.
